title: Fallback signals — refactor EntryEngine + add PULLBACK signal
acceptance_criteria: FallbackSignalEmitter subscribes to straddle.values Redis stream using consumer group 'fallback-signals'/'primary'

Emits SCHEDULED signal at SCHEDULED_ENTRY_TIME (env, default '09:17') as a signal published to signals.generated: {signal_type:'SCHEDULED', signal_id, underlying:'NIFTY', atm_strike, spot, straddle_value, vix, adjusted_probability:1.0, confidence_tier:'HIGH', signal_time}
SCHEDULED signal is emitted at most once per trading day per underlying (tracked in memory, reset on date change)

PULLBACK signal detection: tracks running max straddle_value since last SCHEDULED signal; emits PULLBACK when straddle_value drops by >= PULLBACK_RETRACE_PCT (env, default 3%) from the tracked peak, within the last PULLBACK_LOOKBACK_CANDLES (env, default 8 = 2 minutes) snapshots; adjusted_probability = 0.60, confidence_tier = 'MEDIUM'
PULLBACK deduplication: no new PULLBACK signal within 600 seconds of the previous one

REFACTOR EntryEngine: Remove the global open-position gate (the 'SELECT ... WHERE status=open LIMIT 1' DB query). That check is now per-personality in the Stage 2 filter (T-26). Keep the time-window gate and blocked-date gate in EntryEngine ONLY for backward-compatibility if EntryEngine is still used (it will be deprecated after T-27 wires everything through the router)
IMPORTANT: EntryEngine is NOT removed in this task — it stays as-is except for the global open-position gate removal. Full deprecation/replacement happens after T-27. Document this clearly.
Unit tests: SCHEDULED signal fires at the correct time; PULLBACK fires correctly with known snapshot history; dedup windows work; neither fires outside trading hours
Integration test: FallbackSignalEmitter + real Redis — SCHEDULED signal appears in signals.generated stream within 1 snapshot interval of crossing the entry time
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
